content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

November 6, 1995

Brant J. Bordsen
Rich, Fudge, Morris & Sanbrook
P.O. Box "A"
Marysville, CA 95901

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-347

Dear Mr. Bordsen:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
John Dionne, member of the Live Oak City Council under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTIONS

1) As a volunteer member of the Live Oak Fire Department,
may Councilmember Dionne participate in city council decisions
which may affect the Live Oak Fire Department?

2) Is Councilmember Dionne precluded from receiving
compensation as a volunteer with the Live Oak Fire Department?

CONCLUSIONS

1) Salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem
received from a state, local or federal government agency is
expressly exempted from the definition of "income" for purposes of
the Act. Consequently, since the hourly compensation
Councilmember Dionne receives is in the form of governmental
salary, the payments would not create a conflict of interest with
respect to decisions affecting the Live Oak Fire Department.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18995. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Advice File No. A-95-347
Page 2


     2)  Councilmember Dionne is not precluded from receiving his
hourly compensation from the Live Oak Fire Department.   Note,
however, that our advice is limited to the Political Reform Act.
You may wish to contact the Attorney General's office with respect
to other provisions of the law such as Government Code 1090 and
the law governing incompatible public offices.

                              FACTS

     Councilmember John Dionne is a volunteer fireman for the Live
Oak Fire Department (the "department").  Although he is a
volunteer fireman, he receives $1.70 per hour and $1.00 per call
for overnight stays.  Occasionally the city council may consider
decisions which affect the department, including its volunteers.

                             ANALYSIS

     Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in, or using their official position to influence a
governmental decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest.

     An official has a financial interest in a governmental
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the official, or on a member of the official's immediate
family, or on:

          (a)  Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

                            * * *

          (c)  Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

                    (Section 87103(a) and (c).)

     The Live Oak Fire Department is a local government agency.
(Section 82041.)  Salary from a state, local or federal government
agency is expressly exempted from the definition of "income" for
purposes of the Act.  (Section 82030(b)(2).)  Thus, the hourly
compensation that Mr. Dionne receives from the district is salary
from a government agency and is thus not "income" as used above.
(Cornell Advice Letter, No. A-91-212.)  In addition, because a

Advice File No. A-95-347
Page 3


local government agency is not an organization or enterprise
operated for profit, it is not a business entity as defined by the
Act.

Thus, absent some other disqualifying financial interest in a
decision as set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial
effect on the councilmembers personally resulting from a
governmental decision, as set forth in Regulation 18702.1(a)(4),
Councilmember Dionne may participate in city council decisions
which may affect the department and is not precluded from
continuing to receive his volunteer hourly wage from the
department.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

SGC/JET/jt
